era_context: Mid-8th century BCE: Tiglath-Pileser III's Assyrian Empire is expanding aggressively. The Zhou Dynasty in China is fragmented into competing states. Greek city-states are consolidating their political structures. Classic Maya civilization is flourishing in Mesoamerica.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the Americas outside Mesoamerica

events.title: Tiglath-Pileser III's Levantine Campaigns (Continued)
events.description: Tiglath-Pileser III continues his military campaigns in the Levant as part of his broader imperial expansion strategy. The campaigns involve conquest of local kingdoms and implementation of mass deportation policies. These actions establish Assyrian hegemony over the region and create a system of tributary states.
events.certainty_note: Tiglath-Pileser III's reign is well-documented, but specific year-by-year dating within his reign (745–727 BCE) has uncertainty of ±1–2 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: 758 BCE is extremely poorly documented outside the Near East. The only detailed contemporary sources are Assyrian royal inscriptions. The historical record for this year is almost entirely dependent on Assyrian sources, which focus on military and administrative achievements. Events in other regions are unrecorded or preserved only in much later sources. This creates a severe geographic bias in the surviving historical record.
